sound_type: deep 'whooo' pumping -- single low-frequency notes at 300-400 Hz, spaced every 2-4 seconds, measured and persistent; the most minimal owl vocalization in the engine
ecosystem: Open forest, forest edges, dense vegetation. Winter visitor in western PNW, year-round resident east of Cascades. Cryptic roosting. Communal winter roosts.
season: Winter visitor in western WA (October-April). Year-round east of Cascades. Hooting peaks during breeding season (February-June). Silent outside breeding season.

coordinates.description: Long-eared Owls winter in western Washington. Breeding primarily east of Cascades. Communal roosts in dense conifers.

taxonomy_note.description: First Long-eared Owl entry. Fifth consecutive Strigidae. New genus: Asio (ear-tufted owls). First half of the Long-eared/Short-eared comparison pair (139-140). The most secretive owl in the PNW. Ear tufts are display feathers, not auditory structures.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Strigidae: sixth entry total. Five consecutive at 135-139.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Asio from Latin 'horned owl' or 'eagle owl.' otus from Greek 'eared.' Both references to the ear tufts, which are not ears.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Asio -- first entry. New genus. Includes Long-eared (A. otus) and Short-eared (A. flammeus) owls. Different from Bubo (GHO) and Strix (Barred/Spotted).
taxonomy_note.order_note: Strigiformes. Sixth entry. Five consecutive at 135-139.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Strigidae continues for fifth consecutive degree. Third genus (Bubo, Strix, Asio).

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 300-400 Hz. Deep, low-frequency single note. Pure and resonant.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Single 'whooo' note at 300-400 Hz. Clean, pure tone.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual note: 0.5-1.0 seconds. Inter-note interval: 2-4 seconds. Calling bouts: 1-15+ minutes.
song_metrics.note_structure: SINGLE NOTE PUMPING: Deep 'whooo' repeated at regular 2-4 second intervals. The simplest owl vocalization in the engine. One note, one rhythm, sustained.
song_metrics.note_types: Territorial hoot (deep single 'whooo'), catlike 'waow' alarm, female begging call, nestling begging.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: No phrase structure. Single notes at regular intervals. Pumping rhythm: whooo... whooo... whooo...
song_metrics.timbre: Pure, resonant, clean. Deep bass register without the hoarse quality of the Barred Owl or the booming power of the GHO. A quiet, meditative sound.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Moderate. Carries 200-300 meters in quiet conditions. Designed for dense vegetation communication, not long-range broadcasting.
song_metrics.tempo: Slow and regular. One note every 2-4 seconds. Hypnotic, persistent rhythm.
song_metrics.context: Territorial and mate attraction (breeding season). Primarily February-June. Silent outside breeding season. Strictly nocturnal.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. The deep single hoot is consistent across the species' range.
song_metrics.key_property: DEEP PUMPING 'WHOOO' -- E=4 MEASURED MINIMALISM. Single notes at 300-400 Hz every 2-4 seconds. The most minimal owl vocalization in the engine. Fifth consecutive Strigidae (135-139). Asio genus enters. Most secretive PNW owl. Cryptic roosting. Communal winter roosts. Ear tufts are not ears. First half of Long-eared/Short-eared pair (139-140).

plumage.overall: Mottled brown, buff, and gray. Prominent ear tufts (when raised). Orange-buff facial disc. Yellow-orange eyes. Slender build, elongated proportions.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Long ear tufts (when visible). Orange-buff facial disc. Slender proportions. When roosting, elongated posture with raised tufts resembles a tree branch.
plumage.flight_pattern: Silent flight. Long, broad wings. Buoyant, moth-like flight. Long tail. Slender silhouette distinguishes from stockier Barred and Great Horned Owls.

physical_description.overall: Length: 35-40 cm. Weight: 220-435 g. Wingspan: 86-102 cm. Slender and elongated. Significantly smaller than Barred Owl (500-1050 g) and GHO (900-2500 g).
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Ear tufts (close together on crown, unlike GHO's widely spaced tufts). Orange-buff facial disc. Yellow-orange eyes. Slender build.
physical_description.field_id: Extremely difficult to find. Look for dense conifer stands; check below for pellet accumulations. Listen at night (breeding season) for deep, regular 'whooo' pumping. Communal winter roosts are the best detection method.

nodes.title: Deep Pumping 'Whooo' -- E=4 Minimal Hooting
nodes.description: Single low-frequency notes at 300-400 Hz, spaced every 2-4 seconds. The most minimal owl vocalization in the engine. Measured, persistent, meditative. Breeding season only. Strictly nocturnal.

nodes.title: Most Secretive PNW Owl -- Ghost Owl
nodes.description: Cryptic roosting in dense vegetation. Elongated posture with raised ear tufts. Strict nocturnality. Communal winter roosts (20-50+ birds). Severely underdetected. Present but invisible. The 'ghost owl.'

nodes.title: Ear Tufts -- Display Feathers, Not Ears
nodes.description: The 'long ears' are feather tufts, not auditory structures. Raised for display, camouflage, and communication. Real ears are asymmetrically placed beneath facial disc feathers for 3D sound localization.

nodes.title: Asio Genus Enters -- Ear-Tufted Owls
nodes.description: Third genus in the Strigidae streak: Bubo (GHO, 135-136), Strix (Barred Owl, 137-138), Asio (Long-eared, 139). The Long-eared/Short-eared pair (139-140) are both Asio.

nodes.title: Fifth Consecutive Strigidae (135-139)
nodes.description: Five consecutive owl entries. Three genera. Energy pattern: 4, 5, 4, 6, 4. The longest family streak in the engine extends further.

cross_references.relationship: ASIO COMPARISON PAIR! Long-eared (139, E=4, nocturnal, forest, secretive) and Short-eared (140, E=5, diurnal, grassland, visible). The same genus, opposite habits. Yin and yang of the ear-tufted owls.
STRIGIDAE CONTINUATION! Barred Owl (137-138, Strix) to Long-eared Owl (139, Asio). Third genus enters. From the chaotic E=6 caterwauling to the minimal E=4 pumping. Maximum to measured.
STRIGIDAE STREAK! GHO (135-136, Bubo), Barred Owl (137-138, Strix), Long-eared Owl (139, Asio). Five consecutive. Three genera. The owl family era at full depth.
STRIGIDAE FAMILY! Six entries total. Spotted Owl (4, Strix), GHO (135-136, Bubo), Barred Owl (137-138, Strix), Long-eared Owl (139, Asio). Three genera in the family.
